diagrams.title: Encapsulation in Python Class
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how encapsulation hides internal data using private attributes (_balance, __account_number) while exposing a public interface (deposit, withdraw, get_balance). The lock symbols represent data protection.
elements: BankAccount class box with two sections
Public methods: deposit(), withdraw(), get_balance() - shown with open lock icon
Protected attribute: _balance - shown with single underscore prefix
Private attribute: __account_number - shown with double underscore prefix and lock icon
Arrows showing method calls from outside to class methods
Shield or barrier graphic showing data protection boundary
diagrams.purpose: Explains how encapsulation bundles data and methods while restricting direct access to internal state, enforcing data hiding in Python using naming conventions (_ and __ prefixes).

diagrams.title: Abstraction with Abstract Base Classes
diagrams.description: Shows an abstract DataHandler base class defining a common interface with abstract methods, and concrete implementations (DatabaseHandler, APIHandler, FileHandler) that provide specific implementations.
elements: Abstract class 'DataHandler' at top in italics with abstract method signatures
Abstract methods: connect(), disconnect(), query() - shown with dotted underline
Three concrete subclasses below: DatabaseHandler, APIHandler, FileHandler
Inheritance arrows connecting base class to each subclass
Implementation details hidden inside each concrete class box
User code shown interacting only with abstract interface
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates abstraction by hiding complex implementation details behind a clean interface, allowing users to work with different data sources without knowing their internal workings.

diagrams.title: Abstraction and Encapsulation Together
diagrams.description: Illustrates how both concepts work together: Abstraction defines the 'what' (interface), while Encapsulation protects the 'how' (implementation). Shows a user interacting with a simplified interface while internal details are hidden and protected.
elements: Abstraction layer: Public interface box showing simplified methods
Encapsulation layer: Internal implementation box with private data and methods
Dotted arrow showing user only sees abstraction layer
Solid arrows showing abstraction delegates to encapsulation
Firewall/shield graphic separating user from internal implementation
Python code snippets showing @property decorators and _private conventions
diagrams.purpose: Shows the relationship between abstraction and encapsulation - abstraction handles the 'what' interface while encapsulation handles the 'how' protection, together providing clean, secure, and maintainable code.
